module.title: EXPRESSIONS & CASE
module.subtitle: AS · arithmetic · string funcs · CASE
module.theory:
## Shaping the output
Until now you returned columns exactly as stored. SQL can also **compute new values** inside SELECT and rename them. The list between SELECT and FROM is just a set of expressions.

## Aliases with AS
**AS** gives a column a friendly name in the result. It does not change the table, only the label on the output.
~~~sql
SELECT name AS item FROM gear;
~~~
**WHY** — computed columns get ugly auto-names like `price*qty`. An alias makes the result readable.

> INTEL — The keyword AS is optional (`name item` works) but writing it out is clearer.

## Arithmetic in SELECT
You can do math on columns per row: `+`, `-`, `*`, `/`.
~~~sql
SELECT name, price * qty AS stock_value FROM gear;
~~~
Each row gets its own result. **WHY** — store the raw pieces (price, qty) once, compute totals on demand.

> WARNING — Dividing two integers does integer math: `7 / 2` is **3**, not 3.5. Multiply by 1.0 (`7 * 1.0 / 2`) to force decimals.

## String functions
- **UPPER(s)** / **LOWER(s)** — change case
- **LENGTH(s)** — number of characters
- **SUBSTR(s, start, len)** — slice; positions start at **1**
- **a || b** — glue two strings together (concatenation)
~~~sql
SELECT UPPER(name) AS shout, LENGTH(name) AS chars FROM gear;
SELECT name || ' [' || grade || ']' AS tag FROM gear;
~~~

> WARNING — SUBSTR counts from 1, not 0. `SUBSTR('NEON', 1, 2)` is `'NE'`.

## Rounding numbers
**ROUND(x, n)** rounds x to n decimal places.
~~~sql
SELECT name, ROUND(rating, 1) AS stars FROM gear;
~~~
**WHY** — raw computed numbers can run to many decimals; ROUND makes them presentable.

## CASE — if/else for columns
**CASE** picks a value based on conditions, row by row. It is SQL's if/else.
~~~sql
SELECT name,
  CASE WHEN price >= 1000 THEN 'premium' ELSE 'budget' END AS tier
FROM gear;
~~~
It reads top to bottom: the first WHEN that is true wins; ELSE catches the rest. Always close with **END** (and alias it with AS).

> INTEL — You can chain conditions: `CASE WHEN x>=900 THEN 'A' WHEN x>=600 THEN 'B' ELSE 'C' END`.

## The market
~~~text
gear(id, name, price, qty, rating, grade)
~~~
A black-market cyberware stall: each row is an item with a unit price, units in stock, a 0-5 rating, and a letter grade.

exercises.title: RELABEL THE GOODS
exercises.prompt: From the **gear(id, name, price, qty, rating, grade)** table, return the **name** column but label it **item** in the result (use AS). Return that one column for every row, in table order (no sorting needed).
exercises.setup:
CREATE TABLE gear (id INTEGER, name TEXT, price INTEGER, qty INTEGER, rating REAL, grade TEXT);
INSERT INTO gear VALUES (1,'Neural Jack',1200,3,4.6,'A'),(2,'Optic Mod',640,7,3.9,'B'),(3,'Dermal Plate',300,12,2.8,'C'),(4,'Reflex Booster',1850,2,4.95,'A'),(5,'Skin Weave',420,9,3.2,'B'),(6,'Voice Box',95,20,2.1,'C');
exercises.solution: SELECT name AS item FROM gear;
exercises.hint: SELECT name AS item FROM gear;
exercises.lore: Same chrome, cleaner label. The buyer never reads the serial.

exercises.title: SCREAM THE INVENTORY
exercises.prompt: From the **gear** table, return each item's **name converted to upper case**, labeled **loud** (use UPPER and AS). One column, every row, table order.
exercises.setup:
CREATE TABLE gear (id INTEGER, name TEXT, price INTEGER, qty INTEGER, rating REAL, grade TEXT);
INSERT INTO gear VALUES (1,'Neural Jack',1200,3,4.6,'A'),(2,'Optic Mod',640,7,3.9,'B'),(3,'Dermal Plate',300,12,2.8,'C'),(4,'Reflex Booster',1850,2,4.95,'A'),(5,'Skin Weave',420,9,3.2,'B'),(6,'Voice Box',95,20,2.1,'C');
exercises.starter: SELECT name AS loud FROM gear;
exercises.solution: SELECT UPPER(name) AS loud FROM gear;
exercises.hint: Wrap the column: UPPER(name) AS loud.
exercises.lore: On the night market, the loudest sign moves the most chrome.

exercises.title: STOCK VALUE
exercises.prompt: For every row in **gear**, return two columns: the **name**, and **price multiplied by qty** labeled **stock_value**. Table order is fine.
exercises.setup:
CREATE TABLE gear (id INTEGER, name TEXT, price INTEGER, qty INTEGER, rating REAL, grade TEXT);
INSERT INTO gear VALUES (1,'Neural Jack',1200,3,4.6,'A'),(2,'Optic Mod',640,7,3.9,'B'),(3,'Dermal Plate',300,12,2.8,'C'),(4,'Reflex Booster',1850,2,4.95,'A'),(5,'Skin Weave',420,9,3.2,'B'),(6,'Voice Box',95,20,2.1,'C');
exercises.solution: SELECT name, price * qty AS stock_value FROM gear;
exercises.hint: Compute it in the SELECT list: price * qty AS stock_value.
exercises.lore: Count the till before the till counts you.

exercises.title: ROUND THE RATING
exercises.prompt: From **gear**, return the **name** and the **rating rounded to 1 decimal place**, labeled **stars** (use ROUND). Two columns, every row, table order.
exercises.setup:
CREATE TABLE gear (id INTEGER, name TEXT, price INTEGER, qty INTEGER, rating REAL, grade TEXT);
INSERT INTO gear VALUES (1,'Neural Jack',1200,3,4.6,'A'),(2,'Optic Mod',640,7,3.9,'B'),(3,'Dermal Plate',300,12,2.8,'C'),(4,'Reflex Booster',1850,2,4.95,'A'),(5,'Skin Weave',420,9,3.2,'B'),(6,'Voice Box',95,20,2.1,'C');
exercises.solution: SELECT name, ROUND(rating, 1) AS stars FROM gear;
exercises.hint: ROUND(rating, 1) AS stars — the 1 means one decimal place.
exercises.lore: Four-point-nine-five rounds to five. The reviews never lie that hard.

exercises.title: BUILD THE TAG
exercises.prompt: From **gear**, build one column labeled **tag** that glues each item's **name**, then a space and an opening bracket, then its **grade**, then a closing bracket — for example `Neural Jack [A]`. Use the || operator. One column, every row, table order.
exercises.setup:
CREATE TABLE gear (id INTEGER, name TEXT, price INTEGER, qty INTEGER, rating REAL, grade TEXT);
INSERT INTO gear VALUES (1,'Neural Jack',1200,3,4.6,'A'),(2,'Optic Mod',640,7,3.9,'B'),(3,'Dermal Plate',300,12,2.8,'C'),(4,'Reflex Booster',1850,2,4.95,'A'),(5,'Skin Weave',420,9,3.2,'B'),(6,'Voice Box',95,20,2.1,'C');
exercises.starter: SELECT name AS tag FROM gear;
exercises.solution: SELECT name || ' [' || grade || ']' AS tag FROM gear;
exercises.lore: Every piece on the rack wears its grade like a gang colour.

exercises.title: PRICE TIER
exercises.prompt: From **gear**, return the **name** and a computed label **tier** using CASE: if **price is 1000 or more** the tier is **'premium'**, otherwise **'budget'**. Two columns, every row, table order.
exercises.setup:
CREATE TABLE gear (id INTEGER, name TEXT, price INTEGER, qty INTEGER, rating REAL, grade TEXT);
INSERT INTO gear VALUES (1,'Neural Jack',1200,3,4.6,'A'),(2,'Optic Mod',640,7,3.9,'B'),(3,'Dermal Plate',300,12,2.8,'C'),(4,'Reflex Booster',1850,2,4.95,'A'),(5,'Skin Weave',420,9,3.2,'B'),(6,'Voice Box',95,20,2.1,'C');
exercises.solution: SELECT name, CASE WHEN price >= 1000 THEN 'premium' ELSE 'budget' END AS tier FROM gear;
exercises.hint: CASE WHEN price >= 1000 THEN 'premium' ELSE 'budget' END AS tier.
exercises.lore: Premium chrome, premium prices. Budget chrome, budget regrets.

exercises.title: STOCK CODE
exercises.brief: Slice the first three letters, upper case.
exercises.prompt: From **gear**, build one column labeled **code**: take the **first 3 characters** of each item's **name** and convert them to upper case (use SUBSTR and UPPER). For example `Neural Jack` becomes `NEU`. One column, every row, table order.
exercises.setup:
CREATE TABLE gear (id INTEGER, name TEXT, price INTEGER, qty INTEGER, rating REAL, grade TEXT);
INSERT INTO gear VALUES (1,'Neural Jack',1200,3,4.6,'A'),(2,'Optic Mod',640,7,3.9,'B'),(3,'Dermal Plate',300,12,2.8,'C'),(4,'Reflex Booster',1850,2,4.95,'A'),(5,'Skin Weave',420,9,3.2,'B'),(6,'Voice Box',95,20,2.1,'C');
exercises.starter: SELECT name AS code FROM gear;
exercises.solution: SELECT UPPER(SUBSTR(name, 1, 3)) AS code FROM gear;
exercises.hint: SUBSTR(name, 1, 3) grabs the first three chars; wrap it in UPPER(...).
exercises.lore: Three letters on the crate. The rest is need-to-know.

exercises.title: RESTOCK ALERT
exercises.prompt: From **gear**, return the **name** and a column **status** using a chained CASE on **qty**: when qty is **less than 5** return **'critical'**, when qty is **less than 10** return **'low'**, otherwise **'stocked'**. Two columns, every row, table order.
exercises.setup:
CREATE TABLE gear (id INTEGER, name TEXT, price INTEGER, qty INTEGER, rating REAL, grade TEXT);
INSERT INTO gear VALUES (1,'Neural Jack',1200,3,4.6,'A'),(2,'Optic Mod',640,7,3.9,'B'),(3,'Dermal Plate',300,12,2.8,'C'),(4,'Reflex Booster',1850,2,4.95,'A'),(5,'Skin Weave',420,9,3.2,'B'),(6,'Voice Box',95,20,2.1,'C');
exercises.solution: SELECT name, CASE WHEN qty < 5 THEN 'critical' WHEN qty < 10 THEN 'low' ELSE 'stocked' END AS status FROM gear;
exercises.hint: Order matters in CASE: test qty < 5 first, then qty < 10, then ELSE.
exercises.lore: Two units left on the booster. The fixers are already circling.
